mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that drives wage growth and consumer spending, directly supporting corporate earnings and lifting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power for earnings revisions in subsequent quarterly reports.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts rise while industrial production falls, capital rotates from cyclical manufacturing to consumer-facing sectors, increasing aggregate demand elasticity and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain variance in forward returns beyond individual series effects, or the combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, rising industrial production indicates peak capacity utilization before a downturn, prompting front-running of earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 100, or the signal loses predictive power once the yield curve inverts, indicating a regime shift to contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers market expectations for aggressive Federal Reserve rate hikes, which compress equity risk premiums and discount future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite inflation spikes above 0.3% MoM, or the signal generates positive forward returns in >60% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes indicate expanding consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenue growth and improved earnings guidance, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth decouples from corporate revenue growth, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth accelerates alongside decelerating housing starts, labor-driven consumption offsets housing sector weakness, stabilizing aggregate demand and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k monthly while HOUST falls below 700k.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, positive quarterly GDP growth confirms the end of recessionary dynamics, triggering institutional portfolio rebalancing toward equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity allocations remain defensive despite positive GDP prints, or the signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of post-recession quarters.
